name: Suspicious Windows Shutdown or Restart - Possible Persistence
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: khalaf
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Windows Shutdown or Restart - Possible Persistence
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious system shutdowns or restarts which may be used for establishing persistence or evading detection.

False positives:
- Legitimate shutdowns by update processes
- Scheduled maintenance windows

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of WindowsAuthServer @ H360-DC3, WindowsAuthServer @ H360-TALLY, WindowsAuthServer @ H360FS, WindowsAuthServer @ SRV-APP-H360, WindowsAuthServer @ H360-DC2, WindowsAuthServer @ VM-DMS-Live
when the event QID is one of the following (5001592) Status Shutdown Clean
when the event matches shutdown reason (custom) contains any of (Planned)
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Windows Shutdown or Restart - Possible Persistence on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of WindowsAuthServer @ H360-DC3, WindowsAuthServer @ H360-TALLY, WindowsAuthServer @ H360FS, WindowsAuthServer @ SRV-APP-H360, WindowsAuthServer @ H360-DC2, WindowsAuthServer @ VM-DMS-Live
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001592) Status Shutdown Clean
AND NOT when the event matches shutdown reason (custom) contains any of (Planned)
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Windows Shutdown or Restart - Possible Persistence

Event description: Detects suspicious system shutdowns or restarts which may be used for establishing persistence or evading detection. 
False positives: - Legitimate shutdowns by update processes - Scheduled maintenance windows
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 900 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
